risks: Scope is massive — 8 requirements spanning rendering fixes, real-time data binding, player controls, spatial AI, interactive UI, and research. Even with simplification, this is 3-4 weeks of work and risks context sprawl across initiatives that touch the same files (CanvasOffice.tsx, officeState.ts, renderer.ts).

Real-time data binding + spatial behavior + smooth movement creates a tight coupling triangle. If Zustand updates trigger spatial routing which triggers movement which triggers rendering, any performance issue cascades. Debounce/smoothing strategy needs to be right on the first pass or everything feels broken.

CEO player-controlled character is a game-within-a-game — WASD input, click-to-move, camera follow, collision detection. This is a significant feature that could easily consume as much effort as the entire spatial behavior system.
over_engineering_flags: Requirement 8 (Research & Future) asks to 'plan day/night cycle, weather, achievements' — this is speculative design work that produces documents nobody reads. Reduce to a lightweight research note, not a full design spec.

Full context-aware spatial routing (requirement 5) with per-tool room mapping is complex. A simpler version — agents go to their desk when coding, meeting room when in multi-agent sessions, break room when idle — covers 90% of the visual value without needing a tool-to-room routing table.

Clickable rooms showing 'room activity summary' (requirement 7) implies a new UI component with aggregated state. Clickable agents and desks are high-value; clickable rooms can be a tooltip, not a panel.
challenges.recommendation: Simplify (spatial routing to 4 simple rules instead of full tool-mapping; research requirement as a brief note file not a design doc; clickable rooms as tooltip not panel). Still delivers all 8 requirements but at ~60% of the complexity.

initiatives.title: Fix Z-Sort and Rendering Order
initiatives.user_scenario: Characters no longer clip behind chairs they should be in front of — the office looks correct from all angles.
initiatives.scope: Fix FurnitureInstance.zY in layoutSerializer.ts so up-facing chairs render behind seated characters. Audit all ZDrawable sorting in renderer.ts for edge cases. Verify fix across all 25 seat positions.
initiatives.verify: npm run type-check && npm run build
definition_of_done: FurnitureInstance.zY correctly computed in layoutSerializer.ts so characters in up-facing chairs render in front
All 25 seats visually verified — no character-behind-furniture clipping
No regressions in existing z-sort for desks, monitors, and other furniture

initiatives.title: Bind Game Characters to Real System State
initiatives.user_scenario: When an agent starts a session, the CEO sees that agent's sprite change behavior in the game — typing when coding, reading when reviewing, idle when no session is active.
initiatives.scope: Replace the mock/random agent state mapping in GamePage.tsx with real Zustand store bindings. Map session states to CharacterState. Add debounce/smoothing layer so rapid state changes don't cause jittery sprite transitions.
initiatives.verify: npm run type-check && npm run build
definition_of_done: Agent sprites reflect real session state from Zustand store (working/idle/error)
State transitions are debounced — rapid updates don't cause visual jitter
GamePage.tsx no longer uses mock/random agent status generation
Context data (current task name, session info) available on Character object for later use by HUD/panels

initiatives.title: Character Identity, Names, and Status Indicators
initiatives.user_scenario: Each agent sprite has their name floating above them and a small status icon so the CEO can glance at the office and know who is doing what.
initiatives.scope: Add agent name labels rendered above each character sprite in the canvas render pass. Add status indicator icons as a post-character render pass. Match agent genders to persona definitions. Simple canvas fillText for names, small sprite/shape for indicators.
initiatives.verify: npm run type-check && npm run build
definition_of_done: Agent names displayed above character sprites, legible at default zoom
Status indicators visible above heads: spinner (working), zzz (idle), exclamation (error), speech bubble (in discussion)
Agent sprite genders match persona definitions from agent-registry.json
Indicators animate smoothly using game loop, not React effects

initiatives.title: CEO Player-Controlled Character
initiatives.user_scenario: The CEO controls a character in the office with WASD keys or click-to-move, walking around to inspect agents and furniture.
initiatives.scope: Add CEO character to agent-registry.json. CEO is player-controlled — no session state. WASD keyboard input, click-to-move via A* pathfinding, camera follow. Uses existing Character FSM.
initiatives.verify: npm run type-check && npm run build
definition_of_done: CEO character appears in office with distinct sprite
WASD keys move CEO character tile-by-tile with walking animation
Click-to-move sends CEO to clicked tile via A* pathfinding
Camera smoothly follows CEO character
CEO character collides with walls and furniture correctly
CEO has no session-state binding (special-cased as player, not agent)

initiatives.title: Context-Aware Spatial Behavior for Agents
initiatives.user_scenario: When an agent starts a coding session they walk to their desk; when they join a discussion they walk to the meeting room; when idle they wander to the break room.
initiatives.scope: Define room zones as tile-coordinate ranges on existing 32x32 map. Implement 4 routing rules: coding → own desk, multi-agent session → meeting room, infra tools → server room, idle → break room. Add linger timer on Character (game loop). Busy indicator for multi-task agents.
initiatives.verify: npm run type-check && npm run build
definition_of_done: Room zones defined as tile-coordinate ranges (desks, meeting, server, break)
Agents route to contextually appropriate room based on current session type
Agents linger at location 2-5s after short tasks before moving
Movement uses existing A* pathfinding — no teleporting
Busy indicator shown when agent has multiple concurrent tasks

initiatives.title: Smooth Movement and Animation Transitions
initiatives.user_scenario: Agents walk smoothly between tiles with natural speed instead of teleporting.
initiatives.scope: Ensure character movement is interpolated smoothly. All movement timers in game loop. Fix any teleporting behavior. Coordinate with spatial-behavior initiative.
initiatives.verify: npm run type-check && npm run build
definition_of_done: Characters interpolate smoothly between tiles during movement
Walking speed feels natural
All movement timers use game loop deltaTime, zero React useEffect timers
Direction-facing updates smoothly during multi-tile paths

initiatives.title: Enhanced Game HUD Header
initiatives.user_scenario: Above the game canvas, a sleek dark bar shows current date/time, notification badges, and quick-access buttons.
initiatives.scope: Enhance GameHeader.tsx with date/time, notification badges, quick-access buttons. Dark themed HTML bar above canvas. Data from existing Zustand store.
initiatives.verify: npm run type-check && npm run build
definition_of_done: GameHeader shows current date/time
Notification badges show counts for active sessions and pending directives
Quick-access buttons open relevant SidePanel views
Dark theme consistent with game aesthetic
Data sourced from existing Zustand store

initiatives.title: Clickable Agents, Desks, and Rooms
initiatives.user_scenario: The CEO clicks on an agent to see details, clicks on a desk for reports, and hovers over a room for a tooltip of activity.
initiatives.scope: Extend click handler in CanvasOffice.tsx. Click-on-agent opens SidePanel (existing). Click-on-desk shows agent reports. Hover-on-room shows tooltip. Follow getCharacterAt pattern for furniture hit detection.
initiatives.verify: npm run type-check && npm run build
definition_of_done: Clicking an agent sprite opens agent detail in SidePanel
Clicking a desk/furniture item shows associated agent reports
Hovering over a room zone shows tooltip with room name and current occupants
Hit detection follows existing getCharacterAt pattern
No click conflicts (priority: agent > furniture > room)

initiatives.title: Research Game UX Patterns and Future Features Note
initiatives.user_scenario: A concise research note captures patterns from claw-empire and pixel-agents plus a plan for future features.
initiatives.scope: Study claw-empire and pixel-agents for game UX patterns. Produce brief research note covering stealable patterns, day/night feasibility, weather, conversations, achievements. Max 2 pages.
definition_of_done: Research note created at .context/goals/game/research-game-patterns.md
Covers claw-empire and pixel-agents pattern analysis
Includes feasibility notes for day/night, weather, conversations, achievements
Max 2 pages — concise and actionable
